applied_optimizations.name: Use rocBLAS for GEMM via torch.addmm
applied_optimizations.description: Replaced Triton matmul with torch.addmm which uses optimized rocBLAS backend. This reduced GEMM time from ~1.6ms to ~0.94ms.

applied_optimizations.name: Fuse multiplier + LeakyReLU in Triton kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Combined the separate multiply and LeakyReLU operations into a single Triton kernel, reducing kernel launch overhead and achieving 1.004x speedup over reference.

attempted_but_failed.name: Pure Triton matmul
attempted_but_failed.reason: Simple Triton matmul cannot compete with highly-tuned rocBLAS for large 8192x8192 GEMM. Best achieved was 1.39ms vs 0.94ms reference.

attempted_but_failed.name: Rectangular blocks (256x64)
attempted_but_failed.reason: Worse performance (1.71ms) due to suboptimal memory access patterns

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with Triton kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Caused correctness issues due to triton kernel wrapping incompatibility

notes: Key insight: For large GEMMs, use vendor-optimized libraries (rocBLAS) rather than custom Triton kernels.
Triton is effective for fusing elementwise operations to reduce kernel launch overhead.
Pre-transposing weights avoids runtime transpose overhead.
Block sizes should be multiples of 64 for AMD wavefront alignment.
Final approach: torch.addmm (rocBLAS) for GEMM+bias + Triton kernel for fused mul+LeakyReLU.

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trials 2, 3, and 4 all crashed with no output. The agent needs an extremely explicit, copy-paste-ready solution. The key optimization is folding the multiplier into the GEMM via torch.addmm's alpha/be

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 5 crashed with no output, same as trials 2-4. The agent keeps failing to produce any code. Need to provide an extremely explicit, complete copy-paste solution with zero ambiguity.
